channel.title: npj Quantum Materials
channel.description:
Open for Submissions

Publishing high-quality research on quantum materials and condensed matter.

npj Quantum Materials is a fully open-access journal that aims to publish work that significantly advances understanding of the fundamental properties, fabrication and applications of quantum materials.

npj Quantum Materials covers topics including, but not limited to, superconductivity and superconducting materials, correlated electronic physics and materials, topological quantum physics and materials, quantum phenomena in advanced energy materials, condensed matter, and the materials science of advanced quantum technologies.

In addition to original research, the journal welcomes critical Reviews, and thought-provoking Perspectives and Comment articles.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: [5] Multi-Q spin-valley order in twisted WSe 2
item.content:encoded:
npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00884-6

Multi-Q spin-valley order in twisted WSe2
item.dc:title: Multi-Q spin-valley order in twisted WSe<sub>2</sub>
item.dc:creator: Arthur Bril, Nai Chao Hu, Nick Bultinck
Nai Chao Hu
Nick Bultinck
item.dc:source: npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 2026-04-24; | doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00884-6
item.description:
Authors: Arthur Bril, Nai Chao Hu, Nick Bultinck

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Nai Chao Hu, Nick Bultinck

Score 5/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Adjacent interest; multi-Q order physics often parallels CDW and nematicity in kagome systems.

Tags: #multi-Q #order #WSe2

Abstract: npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00884-6 Multi-Q spin-valley order in twisted WSe 2

item.title: [8] Anisotropic magnetoresistance and magnetic field-tunable Weyl nodes in Weyl metal SrRuO 3 thin films

npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00882-8

Anisotropic magnetoresistance and magnetic field-tunable Weyl nodes in Weyl metal SrRuO3 thin films

Wazid Ahmed

Chun-Yen Lin
Chia-Hung Hsu
Guang-Yu Guo
Wei-Li Lee

item.description:
Authors: Uddipta Kar, Akhilesh Kr. Singh, Elisha Cho-Hao Lu, et al.; last authors: Guang-Yu Guo, Wei-Li Lee

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Guang-Yu Guo, Wei-Li Lee

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Directly relevant to Weyl semimetals and magnetic topological materials.

Tags: #Weyl #SrRuO3 #topological

Abstract: npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00882-8 Anisotropic magnetoresistance and magnetic field-tunable Weyl nodes in Weyl metal SrRuO 3 thin films

item.title: [6] How pairing mechanism dictates topology in valley-polarized superconductors with Berry curvature

npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 15 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00878-4

How pairing mechanism dictates topology in valley-polarized superconductors with Berry curvature

item.dc:creator: Julian May-Mann, Tobias Helbig, Trithep Devakul
Tobias Helbig
Trithep Devakul

item.description:
Authors: Julian May-Mann, Tobias Helbig, Trithep Devakul

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Tobias Helbig, Trithep Devakul

Score 6/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Theoretical exploration of Berry curvature and topology relevant to interpretation of topological materials.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #topology #superconductivity

Abstract: npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 15 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00878-4 How pairing mechanism dictates topology in valley-polarized superconductors with Berry curvature
